tactical_shape: sterile first half before turning up the dial after the break
head-spinning interplays in the second half
Ritsu Doan creating from the right to spark the opener
standout_players: Daizen Maeda — recalled Celtic forward, took his chance with an unerring finish
Ritsu Doan — woke up and created the opener with a rapier-like pass
Ayase Ueda — held the ball cleverly in the build-up
Zion Suzuki — increasingly busy, made a stretching save and got a touch to Elanga's shot onto the woodwork
Keita Nakamura — late first-half shot forced a stretching save
Koki Ogawa — substitute who scooped over a late chance
home_read.momentum: Already through as runners-up and heading into a mouthwatering last-32 clash with Brazil, with confidence drawn from recent history and a belief they can compete with anyone.

tactical_shape: started slowly in a strangely open but low-pulse first half
finished the stronger in the second half
relied on Viktor Gyökeres’s selfless running and centre-forward work to create the equaliser
standout_players: Anthony Elanga — scored with a stunning long-range strike
Viktor Gyökeres — outstanding performance with his back to goal, hard yards throughout
Alexander Isak — quiet overall but nearly won it with a late near-post flick that hit the bar
Jacob Widell Zetterström — surprise choice in goal, made a stretching save
fatigue_injuries: Isak Hien — apparent hamstring injury, with longer-term repercussions possible
away_read.momentum: Qualified for the last 32 and ended the game stronger, though the Hien injury and the uncertainty over their next opponent leave some complications.
